channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: A Job That Changed Me: Life Behind the Counter at a 1990s Photo Lab
item.description: The article recounts the author's part-time job at Rabbit Photo, a one-hour photo lab in the 1990s, and the unique experiences gained from working there. It highlights the contrast between the era's f...

item.title: Experts analyze potential impact of Egypt's agricultural deal
item.description: The article discusses the potential implications of a proposed agricultural agreement between Egypt and Tanzania, focusing on how such a deal could transform farming practices in the region. While the...

item.title: Senate impeachment court issues subpoenas for Sara Duterte's financial records
item.description: The Senate impeachment court has issued subpoenas for the bank and tax records of Vice President Sara Duterte, with the decision prepared days before the trial began. Impeachment court spokesperson Re...

item.title: Court Dismisses Nwoko’s Pre-Election Suit Over Legal Filing Issues
item.description: The Federal High Court in Abuja has dismissed Senator Ned Nwoko's lawsuit against former Delta State Governor Ifeanyi Okowa's APC senatorial ticket. Justice Mohammed Umar ruled the case was fundamenta...

item.title: Strained Canada-U.S. Relations Under Carney and Trump
item.description: The article discusses the deteriorating relationship between Canada and the United States under the leadership of Prime Minister Justin Trudeau (represented by Commerce Secretary Mark Carney) and Pres...

item.title: Tanga City and EACOP agree to build new health centre with 1 billion shillings investment
item.description: The East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) project has reached a significant milestone as Tanga City officials signed an agreement to establish a new health centre funded by a 1 billion Tanzanian shi...

item.title: Apps for US Troops Contain Foreign Code from Adversaries
item.description: A study by Purdue University, West Point, and Florida International University found that over 12.5% of mobile apps marketed to US military personnel contain code from foreign entities, including Chin...

item.title: Midlife Cyclists' Guide to Enhancing Health and Performance Through Lifestyle Adjustments
item.description: This article provides a comprehensive guide for midlife cyclists seeking to maintain their fitness and overall health. The author, Mark Bailey, shares his personal journey of adapting his training and...

item.title: Federal Reserve delays tightening monetary policy amid cooling inflation
item.description: The article discusses the easing pressure on Federal Reserve chairman Kevin Warsh to implement tighter monetary policies following the June Consumer Price Index (CPI) report. The data showed a decline...

item.title: Philippines Condemns China's Unlawful Actions in Ayungin Incident Injuring Filipino Serviceman
item.description: The National Task Force for the West Philippine Sea (NTF-WPS) condemned China's Coast Guard for 'violent and unlawful' actions that injured a Philippine serviceman near Ayungin Shoal. The incident occ...

item.title: Suspended Police Deputy Faces Multiple Charges Including R14m Gem Theft
item.description: Julius Mkhwanazi, the suspended Ekurhuleni metro deputy police chief, has appeared in court multiple times this year facing serious charges. His latest bail application for alleged theft of R14m worth...

item.title: Senator Robin Padilla Condemns China Coast Guard Actions in West Philippine Sea
item.description: Senator Robin Padilla has strongly condemned the alleged violent actions of the China Coast Guard (CCG) against Philippine Navy personnel during a confrontation at Ayungin Shoal in the West Philippine...

item.title: 28-year-old man charged with murdering former Conservative minister Ann Widdecombe
item.description: A 28-year-old man, Joshua Kerry, has been charged with the murder of Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokeswoman. The incident occurred on 9 July 2026 when Widdecombe, 78,...

item.title: Albertans voice concerns over environmental risks of proposed CO2 pipeline
item.description: Residents near the proposed Pathways carbon storage project in Alberta express concerns about potential environmental impacts. The project aims to transport carbon dioxide emissions from oilsands faci...

item.title: ICPC Investigates Femi Gbajabiamila Over PFIPC Allegations
item.description: The Chief of Staff to the President, Femi Gbajabiamila, appeared before the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) as part of an investigation into the activities o...

item.title: Saudi Arabia to Host 2034 FIFA World Cup with Expanded Format and Global Participation
item.description: The 2034 FIFA World Cup, set to be hosted by Saudi Arabia, marks a historic expansion of the tournament with 48 teams competing across 16 host cities. The decision to award the hosting rights to Saudi...

item.title: CRDB Bank supports youth talents in Tanzania
item.description: The article highlights CRDB Bank's initiatives in developing youth talents across Tanzania, emphasizing their role in sports and education. It mentions Mwana FA's recognition of the bank's efforts, wh...

item.title: Spain Wins 2026 FIFA World Cup Final Against Argentina
item.description: Spain secured their second FIFA World Cup title with a hard-fought 1-0 victory over Argentina in the 2026 final. The match, held in New York, was marked by intense competition and pivotal moments. Arg...

item.title: Taxi Boss Sibanyoni to Testify in Matlala Trial as State Prepares to Present Evidence
item.description: The trial of Vusimuzi 'Cat' Matlala and his co-accused resumes with taxi boss Joe Sibanyoni set to testify as the state's first witness. Sibanyoni, a victim in the case, will provide eyewitness testim...

item.title: Canada's Grocery Inflation Rises, with Tomatoes Seeing Sharp Price Increase
item.description: Statistics Canada's June inflation report reveals that grocery price inflation has outpaced overall inflation for 17 consecutive months. While the annual grocery inflation rate slowed to 3.9% in June ...
